name: Loading of Kernel Module via Insmod
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Pawel Mazur
SIGMA Title: Loading of Kernel Module via Insmod
SIGMA Description: Detects loading of kernel modules with insmod command.
Loadable Kernel Modules (LKMs) are pieces of code that can be loaded and unloaded into the kernel upon demand.
Adversaries may use LKMs to obtain persistence within the system or elevate the privileges.


False positives:
- Unknown

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=11 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%syscall%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%insmod%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%/usr/bin/kmod%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Loading of Kernel Module via Insmod on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event matches devicetype=11 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%syscall%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%insmod%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%/usr/bin/kmod%' AQL filter query
